_comment: Live briefing for Cagle's Chasm, 2026-06-18. Generated from a real engine run against live NWS/SREF/HREF/Open-Meteo ingest. At M0.4 this fetch is swapped for POST /v1/briefing; the render layer is unchanged.
summary: Widespread afternoon and evening thunderstorm activity dominates the window — SREF places thunderstorm probability at 96% over the region. Lightning is Extreme during the exposed approach (1300–1400) and egress (2100–2200); the cave interior shelters the technical span from that hazard. Flash flood is Elevated for the 7-hour cave passage (SREF 58%, HREF 22%); NWS hourly shows precip probability at 52–74% through the window with a stormy pattern. Heat reaches Extreme Caution on the surface approach (NWS heat index 90 °F). Cold/wet remains Minimal — egress temperatures of 71 °F with a damp party are in the safe band. Phases were inferred from the overall window. Verify against the linked NWS sources (WFO MRX) before entry.

phases.applicable: Flash flood (cave interior — surface hazards excluded, FR-14c)
phases.note: Cave interior: surface weather isolation applied; only flash flood evaluated for this span (FR-14c).

hazard_detail.label: Elevated
drivers: SREF P(precip/thunder) 58% — in the 20–60% band with measurable forecast precip
AFD (WFO MRX): excessive-rainfall and flooding discussion concurs
HREF neighborhood P(QPF ≥0.5 in/1 h) 22% — same-day high-res sharpening (FR-7a)
NWS hourly: precip probability 52–74% through the technical span, all convective
hazard_detail.logic: Extreme = active Flash Flood Warning; High = Watch OR SREF ≥60% w/ convective rate; Elevated = SREF 20–59% in 20–60% band OR HREF NEP(≥0.5 in/1 h) 20–39% (Appendix B §16.1).
assumptions: Karst cave: surface HUC-12 delineation proxies for karst recharge; true recharge zone may not follow surface topography (FR-4).

hazard_detail.label: Extreme
drivers: SPC marginal convective risk over the window
AFD (WFO MRX): isolated to scattered afternoon thunderstorm activity
HREF neighborhood P(lightning) 24% ≥ 10% — same-day high-resolution sharpening (FR-7a)
NWS hourly: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely 1300–2100
hazard_detail.logic: Extreme = SREF P(tstm) ≥70% OR SPC Moderate+ during any exposed phase (Appendix B §16.2). Cave interior excluded for the technical span (FR-14c).
assumptions: Lightning applies on the surface approach and egress only; the cave technical span is treated as sheltered from surface convective hazards (FR-14c).

hazard_detail.label: Extreme Caution
drivers: Heat index 90 °F on approach — NWS Extreme Caution band (90–103 °F, FR-15)
NWS point forecast: 77 °F / high humidity at 1300 CDT with pre-storm air mass
Approach exertion under load: effective heat strain runs one category hotter
hazard_detail.logic: Uses NWS Heat Index categories directly (FR-15): Caution 80–90 °F, Extreme Caution 90–103 °F, Danger 103–124 °F, Extreme Danger ≥125 °F. Surface hazard only — heat does not apply in the cave technical span (FR-14c).
assumptions: Approach advisory: effective heat strain runs one category hotter under load (Appendix B §16.3).

hazard_detail.label: Minimal
drivers: Apparent temperature 80 °F on egress (wet-party basis) — above the Elevated band
NWS point forecast: 71 °F at 2100–2200 CDT with 0 mph wind
hazard_detail.logic: Minimal = apparent temp > 60 °F for a wet party (Appendix B §16.4). Elevated band starts below 60 °F. Bands are warmer than dry-cold thresholds because wet clothing loses insulation.
assumptions: Posture assumes the party exits wet (FR-16); discount to Minimal is already in effect at these egress temperatures.
